# PyPIM

{{ pyansys }} {{ PyPI }} {{ codecov }} {{ CI }} {{ MIT }} {{ black }}

[PyPIM](https://pypim.docs.pyansys.com) exposes a Pythonic interface to
communicate with the Product Instance Management (PIM) API.

## What is the PIM API?

The PIM API is a gRPC API, enabling both library and app developers to
start a product in a remote environment and communicate with its API.

The PIM API is intended to be as simple as possible to be adaptable in a variety of
network and software infrastructures. Using this API does not require any
knowledge of its infrastructure. You need only know which product to
start and which API the product exposes. The PIM API itself exposes very few
features and assumes that all the configuration is set on a server.

The PIM API is not intended to manage stateless services, to be a job management
system, or a fully featured service orchestration API. Its purpose is to expose
a minimum feature set for managing service-oriented apps.

## Getting started

To use PyPIM, you must have access to the PIM API.

:::{note}
The PIM API is a work in progress. Even though the API definition and the
PyPIM client are published, the service itself is not publicly exposed.
:::

PyPIM itself is pure Python and relies on [gRPC].

### Installation

The `ansys-platform-instancemanagement` package is tested for Python 3.7 through
Python 3.10 on Windows and Linux.

```
pip install ansys-platform-instancemanagement
```

### Configuration

By default, PyPIM is configured externally instead of via code. Anywhere in the
local storage, create a configuration file with this format:

```json
{
    "version": 1,
    "pim": {
        "uri": "dns:pim.svc.com:80",
        "headers": {
            "metadata-info": "value"
        },
        "tls": false
    }
}
```

Then, define the environment variable
`ANSYS_PLATFORM_INSTANCEMANAGEMENT_CONFIG` to point to this configuration file.

### Usage

PyPIM is a single module called `ansys.platform.instancemanagement`, shortened
to `pypim`.

To start MAPDL and communicate with PyPIM, use this code:

```python
import ansys.platform.instancemanagement as pypim
from ansys.mapdl.core import Mapdl

if pypim.is_configured():
    with pypim.connect() as pim:
        with pim.create_instance(
            product_name="mapdl", product_version="221"
        ) as instance:
            instance.wait_for_ready()
            channel = instance.build_grpc_channel(
                options=[("grpc.max_receive_message_length", 8 * 1024**2)]
            )
            mapdl = Mapdl(channel=channel)
            mapdl.prep7()
            ...
```

You can also use PyPIM without the `with` statement, as shown in this code:

```python
import ansys.platform.instancemanagement as pypim
from ansys.mapdl.core import Mapdl

if pypim.is_configured():
    pim = pypim.connect()
    instance = pim.create_instance(product_name="mapdl", product_version="221")
    channel = instance.build_grpc_channel(
        options=[("grpc.max_receive_message_length", 8 * 1024**2)]
    )
    mapdl = Mapdl(channel=channel)
    mapdl.prep7()
    ...
    instance.delete()
    pim.close()
```

### Integration

PyPIM can be integrated in PyAnsys libraries to transparently switch to a remote
instance in a suitable environment. This process is described in [Integration](https://pypim.docs.pyansys.com/version/dev/integration.html)
in the PyPIM documentation.

For example, starting MAPDL with PyPIM is as simple as using this code:

```python
from ansys.mapdl.core import launch_mapdl

mapdl = launch_mapdl()
```

[grpc]: https://grpc.io/

modules.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.service
documentation: Service class module.

classes.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.service.Service

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.service.Service.uri
documentation:
Uniform resource indicator (URI) to reach the service.

For gRPC, this is a valid URI following gRPC-name resolution
syntax. For example, https://grpc.github.io/grpc/core/md_doc_naming.html.

For HTTP or REST, this is a valid http or https URI. It is the base
path of the service API.

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.service.Service.headers
documentation:
Headers necessary to communicate with the service.

For a gRPC service, this should be translated into metadata included in
every communication with the service.

For a REST-like service, this should be translated into headers included in
every communication with the service.

documentation:
Build a gRPC channel communicating with the product instance.

# Parameters

configuration: pim configuration
kwargs: list, optional

> Named arguments for gRPC construction.
> They are passed to `grpc.secure_channel` or `grpc.insecure_channel`.

# Returns

grpc.Channel

: gRPC channel ready to be used for communicating with the service.

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.configuration.Configuration.tls

Whether the connection to PIM requires encryption.

If `True`, the `access_token` property is used to create a secure connection.

If `False`, an insecure connection is used.

Raises

InvalidConfigurationError

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.configuration.Configuration.from_environment
documentation:
Create a PyPIM configuration based on the environment configuration.

Before calling this method, {func}`~is_configured()` should be called to check if
the environment is configured to use PyPIM.

The environment configuration consists in setting the environment variable
`ANSYS_PLATFORM_INSTANCEMANAGEMENT_CONFIG` to the path of the PyPIM
configuration file. The configuration file is a simple JSON file containing
the URI of the PIM API and the headers required to pass information.

The configuration file format is:

```json
{
    "version": 1,
    "pim": {
        "uri": "dns:pim.svc.com:80",
        "headers": {
            "metadata-info": "value"
        },
        "tls": false
    }
}
```

# Returns

Configuration

: PyPIM configuration settings.

# Raises

NotConfiguredError

: The environment is not configured to use PyPIM.

InvalidConfigurationError

: The configuration is invalid.

Configuration for the PIM client.

Raises:
    InvalidConfigurationError: configuration file is not a well formatted json file
    InvalidConfigurationError: version is not supported
    InvalidConfigurationError: a key is missing in the configuration file

Returns:
    Configuration: settings to configure the PIM client

modules.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.definition
documentation: Definition class module.

classes.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.definition.Definition

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.definition.Definition.name
documentation:
Name of the definition.

This name is assigned by the server and always starts with `"definitions/"`.
This name is arbitrary. You should not rely on any static value.

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.definition.Definition.product_name
documentation:
Name of the product.

This is the name of the product that can be started. For example, `"mapdl"` or
`"fluent"`.

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.definition.Definition.product_version
documentation:
Version of the product.

This is a string describing the version.
When the product is following the release process for the Ansys unified installation,
the version is three digits, such as `221`.

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.definition.Definition.available_service_names
documentation:
List of the available service names.

If the product exposes a gRPC API, the service is named `grpc`.
If the product exposes a REST-like API, the service is named `http`.
Custom entries might also be listed, either for sidecar services or
other protocols.

Provides a definition of a product that can be started using the PIM API.

The definition is a static object describing a product that can be started remotely.

classes.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.exceptions.NotConfiguredError
documentation:
Indicates an attempt was made to use PyPIM without the mandatory configuration.

Consider calling {func}`~is_configured()` before using {func}`~connect()`.

Indicates an attempt was made to communicate with an instance that is not yet ready.

Consider calling {func}`~Instance.wait_for_ready`
or checking {attr}`~Instance.ready` before use.

classes.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.exceptions.UnsupportedProductError

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.exceptions.UnsupportedProductError.__init__
documentation: Construct the error from the unsupported product, version, or both.

class_variables.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.exceptions.UnsupportedProductError.product_name
documentation: Name of the requested product.

class_variables.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.exceptions.UnsupportedProductError.product_version
documentation: Version of the requested product.

Indicates that the product or version is not supported by the remote server.

This error is raised when trying to start a product that does not contain
any matching definition in the remote server.

You can try to lift some of the constraints, such as the version constraint.

classes.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.exceptions.RemoteError

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.exceptions.RemoteError.__init__
documentation: Construct the error from the grpc exception.

class_variables.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.exceptions.RemoteError.call
documentation: Failed gRPC call.

Indicates a remote request failed.

When this error is raised, the `__cause__` member contains the original :class:~`grpc.Call`.
TODO: We should likely have more specialized versions, but for now
let's focus on improving the messages coming from the remote

modules.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.interceptor
documentation:
Interceptor that adds headers to outgoing requests.

This is a direct adaptation of the official gRPC example, but it supports multiple headers.
https://github.com/grpc/grpc/tree/master/examples/python/interceptors/headers

functions.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.interceptor.header_adder_interceptor
documentation:
Interceptor adding a set of headers to the requests.

Parameters
----------
headers : Sequence[Tuple[str, str]]
    List of metadata to inject.

modules.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement
documentation: Entry point for the PIM Python client library.

functions.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.connect
documentation:
Create a PyPIM client based on the environment configuration.

Before calling this method, {func}`~is_configured()` should be called to check if
the environment is configured to use PyPIM.

The environment configuration consists in setting the environment variable
`ANSYS_PLATFORM_INSTANCEMANAGEMENT_CONFIG` to the path of the PyPIM
configuration file. The configuration file is a simple JSON file containing
the URI of the PIM API and the headers required to pass information.

The configuration file format is:

```json
{
    "version": 1,
    "pim": {
        "uri": "dns:pim.svc.com:80",
        "headers": {
            "metadata-info": "value"
        },
        "tls": false
    }
}
```

# Returns

Client

: PyPIM client, which is the main entry point to using this library.

# Raises

NotConfiguredError

: The environment is not configured to use PyPIM.

InvalidConfigurationError

: The configuration is invalid.

# Examples

> ```pycon
> >>> import ansys.platform.instancemanagement as pypim
> >>> if pypim.is_configured():
> >>>     client = pypim.connect()
> >>>     # use the client
> >>>     client.close()
> ```
>
> ```pycon
> >>> import ansys.platform.instancemanagement as pypim
> >>> if pypim.is_configured():
> >>>     with pypim.connect() as client:
> >>>         # use client
> ```

channel

    >>> import grpc
    >>> channel = grpc.insecure_channel("127.0.0.0:50001")
    >>> client = pypim.Client(channel)

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.client.Client.list_definitions
documentation:
Get the list of supported product definitions.

# Parameters

product_name

: Filter by product name if provided. The default is `None`.

product_version

: Filter by product version if provided. The default is `None`.

timeout

: Set a timeout in seconds for the request if provided. The default
  is `None`.

# Returns

list

: List of supported product definitions.

# Examples

> ```pycon
> >>> import ansys.platform.instancemanagement as pypim
> >>> client = pypim.connect()
> >>> for definition in client.list_definitions(product_name="mapdl"):
> >>>     print(f"MAPDL version {definition.version} is available on the server.")
>     MAPDL version 221 is available on the server.
> ```

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.client.Client.list_instances
documentation:
List the existing instances.

# Parameters

timeout

: Maximum time in seconds for the request. The default is `None`.

# Returns

list

: List of instances.

# Examples

> ```pycon
> >>> import ansys.platform.instancemanagement as pypim
> >>> client = pypim.connect()
> >>> for instance in client.list_instances():
> >>>     status = "ready" if instance.ready else "not ready"
> >>>     print(f"The instance {instance.name} is {status}.")
>     The instance instances/mapdl-221-yAVne0ve is ready
> ```

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.client.Client.create_instance
documentation:
Create a remote instance of a product based on its name and optionally its version.

This effectively starts the product in the backend, according to the backend configuration.

The created instance will not yet be ready to use. You must call
{func}`~Instance.wait_for_ready()` to wait for the instance to be ready.

# Parameters

product_name

: Name of the product to start. For example, `mapdl`.

product_version

: Version of the product. For example, `"222"`. The default is `None`.

requests_timeout

: Maximum time for each request in seconds. The default is `None`.

# Returns

Instance

: Instance of the product.

# Raises

UnsupportedProductError

: The product or the selected version is not available remotely.

# Examples

> ```pycon
> >>> import ansys.platform.instancemanagement as pypim
> >>> client = pypim.connect()
> >>> instance = client.create_instance(product_name="mapdl")
> >>> instance.wait_for_ready()
> >>> print(instance.services)
> >>> instance.delete()
>     {'grpc': Service(uri='dns:10.240.4.231:50052', headers={})}
> ```

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.client.Client.get_instance
documentation:
Get a remote product instance by name.

# Parameters

name: str

: Name of the instance to get. This name is assigned by the server and
  always start with `instances/`. You should not rely on any static value.
  For example, the name assigned to the instance might be `instances/mapdl-a25g813`.

timeout

: Maximum time in seconds for the request. The default is `None`.

# Returns

Instance

: A remote instance.

# Raises

> InstanceNotFoundError
>
> : The instance does not exist.

Provides a high-level client object for interacting with the PIM API.

This class exposes the methods of the PIM API.

modules.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.instance
documentation: Instance class module.

classes.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.instance.Instance

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.instance.Instance.definition_name
documentation: Name of the definition that created this instance.

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.instance.Instance.name
documentation:
Name of the instance.

This name is assigned by the server and always starts with `"instances/"`.

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.instance.Instance.ready
documentation:
Whether the instance is ready.

If `True`, the `services` property contains the list of entry points
exposed by the instance.

If `False`, the `status_message` property contains a human-readable
reason.

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.instance.Instance.status_message
documentation:
Status of the instance.

Human-readable message describing the status of the instance.
This property is always filled when the instance is not ready.

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.instance.Instance.services
documentation:
List of entry points exposed by the instance.

This property is only filled when the instance is ready.
If the instance exposes a gRPC API, it is named `grpc`.
If the instance exposes a REST-like API, it is named `http`.

It may contain additional entries for custom scenarios such as sidecar services
or other protocols.

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.instance.Instance.__init__
documentation: Create an Instance.

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.instance.Instance.__exit__
documentation: Delete the instance when used in a `with` statement.

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.instance.Instance.__eq__
documentation: Test for equality.

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.instance.Instance.__repr__
documentation: Python callable representation.

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.instance.Instance._create
documentation:
Create a product instance from the given definition.

# Parameters

timeout

: Time in seconds to create the instance. The default is `None`.

# Returns

Instance

: Product instance.

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.instance.Instance.delete
documentation:
Delete the remote product instance.

# Parameters

timeout

: Time in seconds to delete the instance. The default is `None`.

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.instance.Instance.update
documentation:
Update the instance information from the remote status.

# Parameters

timeout

: Time in seconds to update the instance. The default is `None`.

# Raises

InstanceNotFoundError

: The instance was deleted.

RemoteError

: Unexpected server error.

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.instance.Instance.wait_for_ready
documentation:
Wait for the instance to be ready.

After calling this method, the instance services are filled and ready to
be used.

# Parameters

polling_interval

: Time in seconds to wait between each request. The default is `0.5`.

timeout_per_request

: Timeout in seconds for each request. The default is `None`.

# Raises

InstanceNotFoundError

: The instance was deleted.

RemoteError

: Unexpected server error.

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.instance.Instance.build_grpc_channel
documentation:
Build a gRPC channel to communicate with this instance.

The instance must be ready before calling this method.

# Parameters

service_name

: Custom service name. The default is `grpc`.

kwargs: list

: Named argument to pass to the gRPC channel creation.

# Returns

grpc.Channel

: gRPC channel preconfigured to work with the instance.

# Raises

InstanceNotReadyError

: The instance is not yet ready.

UnsupportedServiceError

: The instance does not support the service.

# Examples

> ```pycon
> >>> import ansys.platform.instancemanagement as pypim
> >>> from ansys.mapdl.core import Mapdl
> >>> pim=pypim.connect()
> >>> instance = pim.create_instance(product_name="mapdl", product_version="221")
> >>> instance.wait_for_ready()
> >>> channel = instance.build_grpc_channel(
> >>>     options=[("grpc.max_receive_message_length", 8*1024**2)]
> >>> )
> >>> mapdl = Mapdl(channel=channel)
> >>> print(mapdl)
> >>> instance.delete()
>     Product:             Ansys Mechanical Enterprise
>     MAPDL Version:       22.1
>     ansys.mapdl Version: 0.61.2
> ```
>
> If the service exposes multiple ports, this method can be used to
> connect to another port by providing the service name:
>
> ```pycon
> >>> filemanagement_channel=instance.build_grpc_channel(service_name="filemanagement")
> ```

methods.name: ansys.platform.instancemanagement.instance.Instance._from_pim_v1
documentation:
Create a PyPIM instance from the raw protobuf message.

Parameters
----------
instance : InstanceV1
    Raw protobuf message from the PIM API.
stub : ProductInstanceManagerStub, optional
    PIM stub.

Provides a remote instance of a product.

This class is a context manager and can be used with the `with` statement to
automatically stop the remote instance when the tasks are finished.
